description:
You are a network forensic analyst triaging five packet capture files received during an incident response engagement. Profile the capture files located in /home/ga/Documents/captures/ (http.cap, dns.cap, telnet-cooked.pcap, 200722_tcp_anon.pcapng, and smtp.pcap) to produce a structured forensic inventory report.

For each file, determine:
1. packet_count (integer)
2. capture_duration_seconds (float, rounded to 6 decimal places)
3. average_packet_size_bytes (float, rounded to 2 decimal places)
4. file_size_bytes (integer)
5. encapsulation (string)

The report must also include a summary section with:
- total_packets_all_files: sum of all packet counts
- file_with_most_packets: filename of the file with the highest packet count
- file_with_longest_duration: filename of the file with the longest capture duration
- file_with_largest_avg_packet_size: filename of the file with the largest average packet size

Save the results as valid JSON to /home/ga/Documents/forensic_report.json with this exact structure:
{
  "capture_files": [
    {
      "filename": "http.cap",
      "packet_count": <int>,
      "capture_duration_seconds": <float>,
      "average_packet_size_bytes": <float>,
      "file_size_bytes": <int>,
      "encapsulation": "<string>"
    },
    ...
  ],
  "summary": {
    "total_packets_all_files": <int>,
    "file_with_most_packets": "<filename>",
    "file_with_longest_duration": "<filename>",
    "file_with_largest_avg_packet_size": "<filename>"
  }
}
